title: Caprifolium humile
intro: Caprifolium humile, also known as Lonicera or honeysuckle, is a perennial climbing plant native to Europe and Asia, known for its fragrant white, yellow, or red flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Caprifolium humile include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Caprifolium humile is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Caprifolium humile is classified within the plant family Caprifoliaceae, commonly known as the honeysuckle or elderberry family. This family contains around 450 species of flowering plants. Caprifolium humile is a species of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It is closely related to the genus Lonicera, which includes the more common honeysuckle species. Within the Caprifoliaceae family, Caprifolium humile is typically placed within the tribe Lonicereae.
uses: Caprifolium humile, also known as the dwarf weigela, is often used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is said to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal uses. parts: Caprifolium humile, the medicinal parts of this plant are typically used in traditional medicine. The leaves and flowers are considered to be the primary medicinal parts.
